Blog

Covering Number

May 7, 2026 • Kaizhao Liu

#

Let $1\leq p < q \leq \infty$. For all $\epsilon\in(0,1)$ and $d\in\NN$,

\[\log N(B_p,\|\cdot\|_q,\epsilon)\asymp_{p,q} \left\{\begin{matrix} d\log\frac{e}{\epsilon^s d} & \epsilon \leq d^{-\frac{1}{s}} \\ \frac{1}{\epsilon ^s}\log(e\epsilon ^s d) & \epsilon \geq d^{-\frac{1}{s}} \end{matrix}\right.,\quad \frac{1}{s}\overset{\text{def}}{=}\frac{1}{p}-\frac{1}{q} .\]

When $p>q$,

H"older Class
